Math

Repost: ftw makes my life easier (PFTD #21 & #22)

December 14, 2025

So basically, I decided to do these daily problems to help myself, and I am just posting ftw problems because I think I'm busy.. Well this is the last time I'll be posting ftw problems. It's mostly the solution wiring (tmi: I write my own solutions if you couldn't tell by my horrible writeup) that takes away the most time, and ftw problems, solutions are like one line most of the times.
I also keep skipping woot because I'm busy. There are a lot of lessons for me to make up lol. I have a physics test tomorrow and I was working on a math project, and this week is a busy week too.

Pftd 21

Problem:

what is the greatest prime factor of 3^7 + 6^6?
Source: FTW(you already know)
Genre: N
Personal Difficulty: Early AMC 12
Hint: factor
Solution: Factor it into 3^6(3+2^6), and it turns into 3^6*67, and 67.... is the prime number if you didn't know, so 67 is the answer

pftd 22

Problem:

One card drawn at a random from a standard pack of 52 cards (with the four suit-spades, hearts, diamonds, and clubs). What is the probability that the card drawn is a heart or and ace? Express your answer as a decimal t the nearest hundredth.

Source: Ftw
Genre: C(calculation)
Personal Difficulty: amc 10 p1
Hint: There's total of 13 cards with different numbers
Solution: it's obviously 16/52 but it's the computation that is hard to do in 45secs. simplify to 4/13 and the answer is 0.31

Comments
